fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 68 is inverted: the clamping block executes when `clamp` is False instead of when `clamp` is True, so clamping is applied when it shouldn't be and skipped when it should be.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (clamping logic) should be entered, but the inverted condition `not clamp` means it is skipped.

spec.expr: y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After the clamping branch executes, y should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].

spec.expr: clamp == False or (y__1 >= min(y0, y1) and y__1 <= max(y0, y1)) or y__1 != y__1 + 0

constraints.intent: When clamp is True and x is outside [x0,x1], y__1 will be out of range and must be corrected by the clamp block.

spec.expr: not clamp or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the final y value must be within the calibration range [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
